subject: Career Education
topic: Careers 8: identity, strengths, and career pathways

teacher_overview.title: Careers 8 Teacher Overview
teacher_overview.subtitle: Identity, strengths, pathways, and practical next steps
teacher_overview.directions: Use this sample pack to review whether the engine can produce useful Careers 8 classroom documents with clear student-facing structure.
teacher_overview.prompt: Students connect personal strengths and interests to possible pathways, then practise asking better questions about work, learning, and community contribution.
success_criteria: Students name strengths using specific evidence.
Students connect interests to possible career clusters or pathways.
Students ask realistic questions about training, work conditions, and community impact.
Students leave with one practical next step.

strengths_inventory.title: Careers 8 Strengths Inventory
strengths_inventory.subtitle: What I notice about how I work, learn, and contribute
strengths_inventory.learning_target: I can identify strengths and explain how they might connect to future opportunities.
strengths_inventory.directions: Choose three strengths that describe you. For each one, give a real example from school, home, sports, work, volunteering, or community life.
strengths_inventory.prompt: A strength is more useful when you can prove it with an example. Avoid one-word answers. Explain what you did, what helped, and where that strength might matter later.

vocabulary.definition: a skill, habit, or quality that helps you contribute
a real example that proves your idea
a possible direction for learning, work, or service

success_criteria: I gave a specific example for each strength.
I connected at least one strength to a possible pathway.
I wrote in a way that sounds honest and specific to me.

career_cluster_cer.title: Career Cluster CER
career_cluster_cer.subtitle: Choose a pathway and support your thinking
career_cluster_cer.question: Which career cluster or pathway seems worth exploring next, and why?
career_cluster_cer.learning_target: I can make a supported career-learning claim using evidence from my strengths, interests, and questions.
career_cluster_cer.directions: Pick one pathway to explore. Use evidence from your strengths inventory, interests, and research questions. Explain why this pathway is worth learning more about.
sentence_starters: A pathway I want to explore is...
One strength that connects to this pathway is...
One question I still have is...
This pathway is worth exploring because...
success_criteria: My claim names a specific pathway or career cluster.
My evidence includes something true about me.
I included at least one question for future research.

career_interview_organizer.title: Career Interview Question Planner
career_interview_organizer.subtitle: Ask better questions before judging a career
career_interview_organizer.prompt: Plan questions for a short career conversation with an adult, guest speaker, family member, community member, or video profile.
columns: What the answer might help me learn
success_criteria: I wrote open-ended questions, not yes/no questions.
I asked about daily work, training, challenges, and advice.
I connected at least one question to my own interests or strengths.

next_step_reflection.title: Careers 8 Exit Reflection
next_step_reflection.subtitle: One thing I learned, one thing I will try next
next_step_reflection.learning_target: I can reflect on my career-learning process and name a practical next step.
next_step_reflection.prompt: What did you learn about yourself or a possible pathway today? What is one realistic next step?
success_criteria: I named one thing I learned about myself or a pathway.
I named one specific next step.
My next step is realistic for a Grade 8 student.
